ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Ghee Rice / Nasi Minyak; mapped to the closest parent class 'rice dish' (FOODON_03311069) as the most defensible ancestor available.
ontology.foodex2_description: Rice-based composite dish — cooked rice prepared with fat and spices
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for Ghee Rice. A000E (composite rice dish) is used as the closest defensible FoodEx2 ancestor. Reviewer should confirm or update when a more specific code becomes available.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Ghee Rice or Nasi Minyak as of April 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Ghee Rice is approximately 200g (about one cup of cooked rice), as commonly plated at Malay food stalls and served alongside curries or side dishes.

nutrient_source.title: Ghee Rice — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Ghee Rice (cr_id G00010332). All per-100g values are taken from the HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 2.8g/100g based on ghee content proportion; monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, and trans fat data not reported in the HPB record and set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Ghee Rice (Nasi Minyak) in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 57 based on basmati rice (GI ~50–58, medium category) as the primary carbohydrate component, consistent with Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283) and the University of Sydney GI Database entry for basmati rice. Ghee and whole spices do not materially raise GI. Glycemic load calculated as: GI × carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 57 × 50.38g (200g serving × 25.19g/100g) / 100 = 28.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 231mg/100g (borderline), partially offset by high potassium 327mg/100g and dietary fibre 3.7g/100g
hypertension.guidance: Sodium is in the borderline range at 231mg/100g; however, the high potassium content (327mg/100g) and dietary fibre (3.7g/100g) partially offset this risk — limit portion to 150–200g and avoid pairing with high-sodium curries or gravies to keep total meal sodium within the 600mg target.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 25.19g/100g from basmati rice base; medium GI (~57); meaningful glycaemic load per serving (~28.7)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ghee Rice carries a medium GI (~57) and a high glycaemic load per standard serving (~28.7), making portion control essential — limit to 100–150g cooked and pair with high-protein, high-fibre accompaniments such as dhal or grilled protein to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from ghee (~2.8g/100g); cholesterol reported as 0mg/100g in HPB source; sodium 231mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat contribution from ghee (~2.8g/100g) is below the high-risk threshold but accumulates with regular consumption — choose preparations with minimal ghee, limit portion to 150g, and balance the meal with unsaturated fat sources and vegetables to support a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory whole spices (cinnamon, cloves, cardamom); saturated fat from ghee estimated at ~2.8g/100g (below 5g threshold)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The whole spices used in Ghee Rice — cinnamon, cloves, and cardamom — provide anti-inflammatory polyphenols that may benefit rheumatoid arthritis; however, limit portion size to reduce saturated fat intake from ghee and avoid pairing with nightshade-heavy curries if nightshade sensitivity is a concern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ghee Rice (Malay: Nasi Minyak; Tamil: நெய் சோறு; Hindi: घी चावल) is a fragrant, spiced rice dish central to Malay and South Asian culinary traditions in Singapore and Malaysia. It is prepared by cooking long-grain basmati rice in clarified butter (ghee) together with whole aromatic spices — typically cinnamon sticks, cloves, cardamom pods, and star anise — along with sautéed onion, garlic, ginger, and pandan leaf, then simmering in water or light stock until the rice is fluffy and fully infused with the spice aromatics. The result is a rich, buttery, golden-hued rice with a delicate floral and spiced fragrance, slightly firmer texture than plain steamed rice, and a mildly savoury flavour profile. Ghee Rice is a staple at Malay weddings, festive occasions, and everyday lunch and dinner settings, typically served alongside lamb or chicken curries, dhal, acar (pickled vegetables), and hard-boiled eggs. Nutritionally, per 100g it provides 164 kcal, 6.5g protein, 4.21g fat (of which approximately 2.8g is saturated from ghee), 25.19g carbohydrate, 3.7g dietary fibre, and 231mg sodium. The high potassium content (327mg/100g) partially offsets the borderline sodium level. Clinically, the medium glycaemic index (~57, basmati rice basis) and meaningful glycaemic load per serving (~28.7) require portion control for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The saturated fat from ghee warrants moderation for cardiovascular health. The anti-inflammatory spice profile is a positive attribute for inflammatory conditions such as rheumatoid arthritis.
common_user_queries: Is nasi minyak suitable for diabetics?
How many calories in ghee rice?
What spices are in ghee rice?
Ghee rice vs plain rice nutrition
Can I eat ghee rice with high blood pressure?
What is nasi minyak made of?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010332, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). GI estimated from basmati rice analogue in the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008, which is the closest approved GI source for this dish. Saturated fat estimated from ghee proportion; all other fatty acid sub-fractions set to -1 as not reported in the HPB record.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, water_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: detailed fatty acid sub-fractions, micronutrient vitamins, and moisture data for Ghee Rice are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (G00010332) as of April 2026, and no supplementary approved source (USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA) contains an exact match for this composite dish. 2. cholesterol_mg is reported as 0.0 in the HPB source; this is accepted as confirmed zero per the HPB record despite ghee being a dairy fat — the HPB analytical value is used as-is. 3. saturated_fat_g of 2.8g/100g is estimated from the ghee content proportion in the dish formulation, consistent with the input record's rheumatoid arthritis health suitability note; this is an approximation and should be confirmed by direct laboratory analysis. 4. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate based on basmati rice (University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008); no direct GI measurement for Ghee Rice exists in any approved source. 5. FoodEx2 code A000E is used as the closest defensible ancestor for a composite rice dish prepared with fat and spices; a more specific code should be assigned when available. 6. Chinese names (黄油饭 / 黃油飯) are descriptive translations used in Singapore Chinese-language contexts; the dish is primarily identified by its Malay name Nasi Minyak. 7. ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type corrected to 'closest_parent' (from 'broader') to conform to the allowed enumeration values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'] as required by the scoring model.
